description:
## The bomb

`tests/common/migrate.rs::legacy_clone()` produces its "pre-XDG legacy clone" fixture by running real `bl init`:

```rust
// tests/common/migrate.rs (lines 27-64)
/// Set up a fresh bare remote and a legacy-layout clone of it. The
/// returned clone is pre-XDG: `.balls/config.json` on `main`, a
/// `.balls/state-repo/` runtime checkout, and balls entries in
/// `.gitignore`. ...
pub fn legacy_clone(home: &Path, sub: &str) -> (Repo, PathBuf, String) {
    let remote = new_bare_remote();
    ...
    bl_xdg(&clone_root, home).arg("init").assert().success();
    push(&clone_root);
    ...
}
```

The docstring promises pre-XDG state. The mechanism delivers "whatever `bl init` writes today." Today that's still the Phase-1A legacy layout — `src/store_init.rs::Store::init` writes `.balls/config.json`, `.balls/state-repo/`, the `balls: initialize` commit on main, the `.gitignore` entries.

bl-213e (Phase 1B-5) is scoped to "replace `Store::init(&cwd, stealth, tasks_dir)` with `Store::init_xdg(&cwd, stealth, tasks_dir)` on the non-`--bare` path." The moment that lands, `legacy_clone()` silently flips to producing an XDG clone, the helper's docstring becomes a lie, and every test that depends on it stops exercising the legacy path while still passing.

## Affected consumers (`git grep legacy_clone tests/`)

- `tests/conformance_migrate.rs`
- `tests/conformance_migrate_dirty.rs`
- `tests/conformance_migrate_renames.rs`
- `tests/conformance_moved_clone.rs`
- `tests/conformance_prime_migrate.rs`

These tests specifically exercise verbs designed for users on the legacy layout: `bl migrate`, `bl prime --migrate`, `bl repair --rebind-path` against moved-clone state. The legacy path doesn't go away when init flips — it stays load-bearing forever (any user on the released legacy binary will arrive at the new binary with a legacy clone). The tests must keep working against a real pre-XDG fixture.

## Why Phase 1B's existing scope does not cover this

bl-ad75 (Phase 1B-4) addresses `conformance_migrate*.rs` but its description claims:

> These test `bl migrate` (Phase 2, bl-717e), which converts legacy → XDG. They are already largely correct (they hand-construct legacy fixtures and run migrate).

Factually wrong: they don't hand-construct, they call `legacy_clone()` which calls `bl init`. bl-ad75 also does not mention `conformance_moved_clone.rs` or `conformance_prime_migrate.rs` at all, even though both use `legacy_clone()` and test legacy-only verbs.

bl-7997 (Phase 1B-3) is about migrating *XDG-tolerant* test assertions onto helpers — out of scope for the legacy fixture itself.

bl-e802 item 7 (mass migration of init-touching tests) discusses post-flip XDG fixture rewiring, not preservation of the legacy fixture for legacy-targeting tests.

## Fix shape

Replace the body of `legacy_clone()` with explicit filesystem scaffolding that matches §14 of SPEC-clone-layout's legacy post-state, independent of what `bl init` currently does:

- `fs::write(.balls/config.json, Config::default().to_json())`
- `fs::create_dir_all(.balls/{state-repo, local/{claims,lock,plugins}, plugins})`
- materialize `.balls/state-repo/` via `state_repo::ensure` directly (or hand-clone the bare orphan branch)
- amend `.gitignore` with the balls entries
- commit `balls: initialize` on main, push

This makes the helper's behavior independent of `cmd_init`'s implementation choice. The docstring's promise becomes the contract.

## Why file now

Filing as a peer dep of bl-213e (the cmd_init flip). If bl-213e lands first, the bug surfaces as "five test files start passing for the wrong reason" — exactly the bl-fc1d class but at higher blast radius (5 files vs 1). Catching it before the flip is the cheap moment.

## Audit trail

Surfaced by bl-fc1d audit conversation, 2026-05-25.
title: Rework legacy_clone() fixture to be independent of bl init's layout choice (Phase 1B time-bomb)
